description_long: This object is a vintage handheld two-way radio or walkie-talkie, featuring a rectangular olive-green plastic body with a prominent black antenna extending from the top. The front panel is equipped with a metallic speaker grille, a rotary dial, a red push button, and several labeled switches and indicators, all set within a black control area. The sides are plain, with one side showing a small protruding switch or button, while the back is mostly featureless except for a battery compartment cover. The device is compact and designed for portability, likely used for communication in urban or field environments. Its utilitarian design suggests durability and ease of use, with tactile controls for operation. The overall appearance is consistent with mid-to-late 20th-century portable communication devices.
description: A vintage olive-green handheld walkie-talkie with a long black antenna, metallic speaker grille, and tactile control buttons and dials on the front.
description_view_0: Front view shows the speaker grille, rotary dial, red button, and control panel details.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the slim profile and the full length of the antenna.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a plain back with a battery compartment cover.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows a small protruding switch or button and the antenna.
